message:
fix(evaluator): re-check Railway control-plane pack on original curl payloads even after quick-reject

Tightens the original-command-payload re-check that was added for
platform.railway so that two real-world scenarios are handled
correctly:

1) Quick-reject path: the top-level pack-aware quick-reject in
   evaluate_command_with_pack_order_deadline_at_path() short-circuits
   before any pack runs. For curl invocations whose only Railway
   signal lives inside `--data*` (the Authorization header carries
   the bearer token, the GraphQL mutation lives in --data-binary),
   the sanitized command-for-match has the payload masked as inert
   data, the keyword scan misses, and the whole evaluation returned
   "allowed" without the railway pack ever getting a chance to look.
   This left a real abuse path open: an attacker could exfiltrate or
   destroy projects via the Railway public API and dcg's keyword
   scan never noticed.

   Now the quick-reject is gated by
   should_check_original_control_plane_payload_for_any_pack(...) — if
   any ordered pack would re-check the original command for control-
   plane signals, the quick-reject is suppressed and the pack
   evaluation runs.

2) Pack-list filtering paths: the existing
   should_check_original_control_plane_payload() guard was already
   wired into the pack-iteration paths but was triggering on *any*
   masked input, which meant innocuous `echo 'projectDelete with
   RAILWAY_API_TOKEN belongs in docs'` (a comment / doc string)
   would activate the Railway pack on the unmasked text. Tightened
   the guard to require:
   - The sanitized form actually differs from the original
     (something was masked).
   - The pack id is platform.railway.
   - The sanitized command contains "curl" (we only re-check
     executing HTTP clients, not echos / comments / heredocs).
   - The original command contains a Railway API control-plane
     signal: backboard.railway.{app,com}, railway.{app,com}/graphql,
     RAILWAY_API_URL, or RAILWAY_API_TOKEN.

   Documentation, README excerpts, and shell-quoted prose that
   merely mention these tokens but aren't issuing a request stay
   allowed — only authenticated curl calls to the control plane get
   re-inspected.

original_command_contains_railway_api_signal() encapsulates the
signal list so it stays in lockstep with the railway pack's own
patterns; if a future Railway API host appears in the pack's
matchers it should be added here too.

Two tests cover both directions:
- railway_api_mutations_with_token_header_are_not_hidden_by_data_masking
  asserts that curl + Authorization: Bearer $RAILWAY_API_TOKEN +
  --data-binary projectDelete is blocked under platform.railway.
- masked_non_curl_documentation_stays_allowed_for_railway_api_terms
  asserts echo'd documentation strings are still allowed.

Plus an integration test in tests/test_command.rs exercising the
same token-header attack through the dcg CLI binary, verifying the
JSON output reports decision=deny, pack_id=platform.railway,
pattern_name=railway-api-project-delete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
